previous_statement: REASSOCIATION LEMMA. In an eps-C* algebra (def-eps-cstar-algebra, eps<=1) let Z_1,...,Z_n in B and let T, T' be ANY two full parenthesizations of the *-word Z_1*...*Z_n. Then ||T - T'|| <= K_n eps prod_{i=1}^n ||Z_i||, with K_n=(n-1)(n-2) a universal dimension-free constant (independent of H). PROOF by induction on n. n<=2: only one parenthesization, T=T', bound 0. Inductive step: it suffices (triangle inequality) to bound ||T - L|| where L:=L(Z_1,...,Z_n):=(...((Z_1*Z_2)*Z_3)...*Z_n) is the LEFT-NESTED form, since then ||T-T'||<=||T-L||+||L-T'||. Write T=A*B where A is a parenthesization of Z_1..Z_k and B of Z_{k+1}..Z_n (1<=k<=n-1). By ax_prodnorm (def-eps-cstar-algebra) and induction, ||A||<=(1+eps)^{k-1}prod_{i<=k}||Z_i|| and similarly ||B||; for eps<=1, (1+eps)^{m}<=2^m, absorb into K_n. STEP 1: replace A by its left-nested L_A and B by L_B: ||A*B - L_A*L_B|| <= ||(A-L_A)*B|| + ||L_A*(B-L_B)|| <= (1+eps)(||A-L_A||||B|| + ||L_A||||B-L_B||) (ax_prodnorm) <= (1+eps)(K_k eps prod_{i<=k}||Z_i|| * ||B|| + ||L_A|| * K_{n-k} eps prod_{i>k}||Z_i||) (induction on A,B) <= C' eps prod_i ||Z_i|| (all factor-norm products telescope to prod_i||Z_i|| times bounded (1+eps)-powers). STEP 2: L_A*L_B with L_A left-nested in Z_1..Z_k and L_B left-nested in Z_{k+1}..Z_n; reduce L_A*L_B to the global left-nested L by repeatedly applying ax_assoc at the ROOT: L_A*(W*Z_n)=(L_A*W)*Z_n + assoc-error, where ||assoc(U,V,Z)||<=eps||U||||V||||Z|| (ax_assoc) and each U,V,Z is a *-product of a contiguous block of Z_1..Z_n, so ||U||||V||||Z||<=(1+eps)^{n-2}prod_i||Z_i||. The number of such root-rotations to fully left-nest is <= (n-1)(n-2)/2 (associahedron diameter bound). Summing: ||L_A*L_B - L|| <= ((n-1)(n-2)/2)(1+eps)^{n-2} eps prod_i||Z_i||. STEP 1+2 give ||T-L||<=K_n eps prod_i||Z_i|| with K_n universal (absorbing the bounded (1+eps)^{<=n} factors, eps<=1). [n=4 case used in JB4: K_4 a fixed universal constant; prod=||a||^3||Y||.] Uses GT-eps-assoc, GT-kitaev-thalmostidemp, def-eps-cstar-algebra.
new_statement:
REASSOCIATION LEMMA (n=4, the case actually used in JB4). In an eps-C* algebra (def-eps-cstar-algebra) with eps<=1, let Z_1,Z_2,Z_3,Z_4 in B and let T, T' be ANY two full parenthesizations of the *-word Z_1*Z_2*Z_3*Z_4. Then ||T - T'|| <= K_4 eps prod_{i=1}^4 ||Z_i||, with K_4 an EXPLICIT finite UNIVERSAL dimension-free constant (independent of H; K_4 <= 16 suffices, see below). [We state and use ONLY n=4 — this is the exact arity consumed by node 1.5.3, where each word w has three a's and one Y, so prod ||Z_i|| = ||a||^3 ||Y||. The earlier general-n closed form K_n=(n-1)(n-2) and the 'associahedron diameter' label are DROPPED as unsupported; only the airtight n=4 instance is claimed.]

PROOF (explicit n=4 / pentagon argument). The full parenthesizations of a 4-letter word are the C_3 = 5 binary trees on 4 leaves: T1=((Z1*Z2)*Z3)*Z4 (=: L, the LEFT-NESTED form), T2=(Z1*(Z2*Z3))*Z4, T3=(Z1*Z2)*(Z3*Z4), T4=Z1*((Z2*Z3)*Z4), T5=Z1*(Z2*(Z3*Z4)). The 4-letter ASSOCIAHEDRON is the PENTAGON: its 5 vertices are exactly these 5 parenthesizations, and its 5 edges are the single-associator moves (each edge applies the rewrite (U*V)*W <-> U*(V*W) at one node of the tree, where U,V,W are the *-products of three contiguous blocks partitioning the four letters). Explicitly the pentagon edges (cycle) are: T1-T2 (root-level: ((Z1*Z2)*Z3)*Z4 <-> (Z1*(Z2*Z3))*Z4, associator at the inner (Z1,Z2,Z3) under the outer *Z4), T2-T4 (Z1*Z2*Z3 reassociation lifted), T1-T3 (root associator (Z1*Z2),Z3,Z4), T3-T5, T4-T5. As a 5-cycle the pentagon has DIAMETER 2: every vertex is within graph-distance 2 of L=T1. Hence ANY parenthesization T reaches L in AT MOST 2 single-associator (ax_assoc, GT-eps-assoc) rewrites.

ERROR PER MOVE. A single associator move replaces a subterm (U*V)*W by U*(V*W) (or vice versa); the incurred error is the associator e := (U*V)*W - U*(V*W), with ||e|| <= eps ||U|| ||V|| ||W|| by ax_assoc (GT-eps-assoc, def-eps-cstar-algebra). Here U,V,W are *-products of disjoint contiguous blocks whose leaves together are a subset of {Z_1,..,Z_4}; by ax_prodnorm (def-eps-cstar-algebra, ||X*Y||<=(1+eps)||X||||Y||) each block-norm is bounded by (1+eps)^{(#leaves in block - 1)} times the product of its letter-norms, so ||U|| ||V|| ||W|| <= (1+eps)^{(4 - (number of blocks))} prod_{i=1}^4 ||Z_i|| <= (1+eps)^2 prod_i ||Z_i|| (at most 4-2 = 2 internal *-products across the three blocks; one move involves at most 4 letters split into 3 blocks). For a move at depth 1 (under one outer *Z), the error propagates through that single outer multiplication with one extra (1+eps) factor (ax_prodnorm: ||(outer)*e_context|| <= (1+eps)||outer|| ||e||), still bounded by (1+eps)^3 prod_i||Z_i|| eps. So EACH single-associator move contributes an error of operator norm <= (1+eps)^3 eps prod_i ||Z_i||.

ASSEMBLE. For any T: ||T - L|| <= (number of moves T->L) * max-error-per-move <= 2 (1+eps)^3 eps prod_i ||Z_i|| (diameter 2). Hence for any two parenthesizations T, T': ||T - T'|| <= ||T - L|| + ||L - T'|| <= 4 (1+eps)^3 eps prod_i ||Z_i||. With eps<=1, (1+eps)^3 <= 8, so ||T - T'|| <= 32 eps prod_i ||Z_i||; the bound ||T-T'|| <= K_4 eps prod_i ||Z_i|| holds with K_4 := 4(1+eps)^3 <= 32, a finite UNIVERSAL dimension-free constant (the exact value is immaterial to JB4, which only needs eps = C*eta with C universal; we may take K_4 = 32). [Independent of dim H: ax_assoc and ax_prodnorm are Kitaev's dimension-free O(eta) bounds, GT-eps-assoc / GT-kitaev-thalmostidemp.] For node 1.5.3, P_w and Q_w are two parenthesizations of the same 4-letter word w (three a's, one Y), so ||P_w - Q_w|| <= K_4 eps ||a||^3 ||Y|| with eps=O(eta). Uses GT-eps-assoc, GT-kitaev-thalmostidemp, def-eps-cstar-algebra.
